parameters.content:
Split Out node breaks ONE item containing an array into MULTIPLE individual items"

"Split Out node is like opening a box and taking out each item individually. When our Code node returns one item containing an array of 5 employees, the Split Out node opens that array and creates 5 separate individual items. "

1 Disable Dot Notation
❓ What is Dot Notation?
In n8n, dot notation means accessing nested fields using a dot (.)
{
  "student.name": "Alice"   ← this is ONE field with a dot in its name
}

2 Destination Field Name
❓ What does it do?
It lets you rename the split field in the output instead of keeping the original field name.
Original field name: students
You set Destination Field Name: student_info

  "student_info": {        ← renamed from "students"
    "roll_no": 1,
    "name": "Alice",
    "class": "A"
  },
  "year": 2024
}

3 Include Binary
❓ What is Binary Data in n8n?
Binary data = non-text files like:

📄 PDFs
🖼️ Images
📊 Excel files
📁 Any file attachment
❓ What does Include Binary do?
When you split items, by default binary/file data is NOT carried to each output item.

Enabling Include Binary = YES means each split item will also carry the binary/file data with it.
{
  "students": ["Alice", "Bob"],
  "report_card.pdf": "<binary file data>"
}

The Include option controls how much context travels with each split item downstream. It helps balance between having enough data to work with and keeping the workflow output clean and efficient.

Scenario	Best Option(Include)
Just process each student's data
No Other Fields

Need school name/year with every student	
All Other Fields

Need only school name but not year
Selected Other Fields
